key_insight: In RLVR, orthonormal LoRA initialization preserves geometry better than common structured initializations, giving the smallest gap to full fine-tuning and improving stability.
engineering_relevance: For parameter-efficient RL on mathematical reasoning tasks, the choice of low-rank initialization can materially affect training robustness, so RLPO/RLMO offer a safer default than standard LoRA, PiSSA, or MiLoRA.
method: The authors analyze LoRA under RLVR and derive that orthonormal initialization minimizes the discrepancy from full fine-tuning.
They propose geometry-preserving orthonormal variants: RLPO and RLMO.
They compare against standard LoRA, PiSSA, and MiLoRA to explain why structured SFT-friendly inits may fail in RLVR.
evidence_signal: Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show the orthonormal initialization stabilizes RLVR training and outperforms standard LoRA, while PiSSA and MiLoRA can underperform.
watch_item: The benefit appears tied to RLVR and task/setup specifics, so implementations should verify orthonormal initialization is preserved throughout training and not assume SFT results transfer directly.
paper_title: Geometry-Preserving Orthonormal Initialization for Low-Rank Adaptation in RLVR
